data.title: SA Asks: Which oncology companies are attractive M&A targets?
data.description:
Which cancer drug developers are attractive takeover targets?

We asked Seeking Alpha analysts BioCGT Investor, ALLKA Research and Zach Hartman for their picks.

BioCGT Investor: Cardiff Oncology (NASDAQ:CRDF), which is on the verge of releasing Phase 2 clinical trial results in first-line RAS-mutated colorectal cancer, comes to mind as a very attractive target for M&A. Onvansertib’s mechanism of action positions it ahead of competitors such as Lumakras and Krazati. If positive, the phase 2 clinical trial results are likely to raise the attention of larger pharmaceutical companies. 

Given its current valuation and interesting developments in the allogeneic CAR-Ts against blood and solid cancers, Allogene Therapeutics (NASDAQ:ALLO) could be a very attractive M&A target for Bristol Myers (NYSE:BMY) or Gilead (NASDAQ:GILD), which already have strong presences in the oncology cell therapies area. 

Janux Therapeutics (NASDAQ:JANX) is developing tumor-activated T cell engagers and tumor-activated immunomodulator platforms. The company has strong collaborations with large pharmaceutical companies; however, the early clinical trial stage of their product candidates and high market cap may make potential acquirers a bit cautious. 

ALLKA Research: Among potential M&A targets in oncology, I would highlight Incyte (NASDAQ:INCY) and its flagship drug Jakafi, which is the "gold standard" in the treatment of myelofibrosis.

I would also not ignore Blueprint Medicines (NASDAQ:BPMC), which is actively developing a pipeline of product candidates aimed at treating solid tumors and breast cancer. In addition, it again raised its 2025 guidance and now expects Ayvakit/Ayvakyt sales to be $700-$720 million.

I also note Legend Biotech (NASDAQ:LEGN) and its treatment Carvykti, which is being developed jointly with Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ). Sales of this highly effective CAR T-cell therapy for the treatment of multiple myeloma amounted to $369 million in Q1 2025, an increase of 135% year-over-year.

Zach Hartman: I know there's a lot of pessimism about the cell therapy space due to challenges with manufacturing, scalability and scrutiny about drug costs. However, in my opinion, this is likely a pendulum that has swung a bit far toward negative sentiment. Adaptimmune (NASDAQ:ADAP) and Autolus (NASDAQ:AUTL) are two companies that catch my attention as commercial stage, generating revenues, and continuing to be valued as having nothing. 

There aren't any recent M&A deals that I've seen supporting strong interest in acquiring cell therapy companies, but the only truism about M&A that I've found in biotech is that almost nothing about these deals is very predictable. Not the buyers, not the sellers, not the price. But the signals we're seeing in 2025 point to more action on the M&A front, and cell therapies are ripe for the plucking at these valuations, in my opinion.

  • Top Biotechnology Stocks
  • Top Pharmaceuticals Stocks

data.title: CDC updates U.S. immunization schedule after RFK Jr. comments on COVID shots
data.description:
The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) has revised its child immunization schedule after Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. said that COVID-19 shots will no longer be recommended for healthy pregnant women and children.

The CDC revised the schedule on Thursday to indicate that children aged six months and older “may receive COVID-19 vaccination” following shared clinical decision-making, which considers the views of healthcare providers as well as those of patients/parents/guardians.

However, the CDC guidance does not appear to reflect that COVID-19 vaccinations are not recommended for healthy pregnant women, as claimed by RFK Jr. early this week. Emily Hilliard, the press secretary of the HHS, said that the CDC vaccine schedule has been revised to remove COVID vaccine recommendation for healthy children as well as pregnant women.

"Under the leadership of Secretary Kennedy, HHS is restoring the doctor-patient relationship. If a parent desires their healthy child to be vaccinated, their decision should be based on informed consent through the clinical judgement of their healthcare provider," she added.

RFK Jr.’s announcement, issued on Tuesday via a 58-second video on X, ran contrary to the CDC’s established process of releasing such guidance following recommendations of the agency’s advisory committee on immunization practices (ACIP).

COVID-19 vaccine makers: Pfizer (NYSE:PFE)/ BioNTech (NASDAQ:BNTX), Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA), Novavax (NASDAQ:NVAX).

Update 3.00 PM EST: Adds comments from HHS

data.title: Nordson raised at Oppenheimer after recovery from cyclical downturn
data.description:
Nordson (NASDAQ:NDSN) +1.7% in Friday's trading as Oppenheimer upgraded shares to Outperform from Neutral with a $260 price target, citing "sturdy positioning" across the company's end-markets, cyclical headwinds that have largely abated, underappreciated portfolio tuning, and increasingly focused cost structure.

Nordson (NASDAQ:NDSN) is "emerging from a relatively nettlesome layering of cyclical headwinds," Oppenheimer analyst Christopher Glynn wrote, but the cycle in advanced technology solutions has "turned with increasing assertion, precision Ag growth is leading broader sector recovery," and medical interventional destocking is "thinning out" with orders growing.

Friday's gains follow a 6.7% surge in the previous session after its FQ2 revenues and Q3 guidance beat estimates, which CEO Sundaram Nagarajan said was "driven by strength in our electronics systems sales and steady growth in nonwovens systems, precision agriculture and medical fluid components."

"Inbound order activity appears to have strengthened, driving backlog higher by 5% Q/Q," and higher than expected margins and stronger organic sales in the ATS segment helped drive the Q2 operational beat, according to D.A. Davidson's Matt Summerville, who rates Nordson (NDSN) as a Buy.

data.title: Why is Summit stock in selloff after late-stage trial data for lead asset?
data.description:
Summit Therapeutics (NASDAQ:SMMT) lost ~29% on Friday, marking its second-biggest intraday decline this year after the Miami, Florida-based biotech signaled a partial success in a Phase 3 trial for its lead drug, ivonescimab, developed with its Chinese partner Akeso (OTCPK:AKESF).

Citing topline data, Summit (NASDAQ:SMMT) said the PD-1/VEGF bispecific antibody in combination with chemotherapy reached the primary endpoint of progression-free survival in the trial named HARMONi, which involved certain patients with non-squamous non-small cell lung cancer.

However, despite a positive trend, HARMONi’s other primary endpoint, overall survival, didn’t indicate a statistically significant benefit, clouding the company’s prospects of seeking U.S. approval for the drug.

“The FDA noted that a statistically significant overall survival benefit is necessary to support marketing authorization, which will weigh into Summit’s considerations regarding the timing of a potential BLA filing,” Summit said.

Edmund Ingham, Seeking Alpha’s Investing Group Leader for Haggerston BioHealth, downgraded SMMT to Sell from Hold in reaction to the readout, which he said had added uncertainty to the drug’s potential U.S. approval.

“Summit Therapeutics Inc.'s HARMONi study met its PFS endpoint but failed to show statistically significant overall survival, casting doubt on U.S. FDA approval prospects,” Ingham wrote.

The analyst also pointed to a decision by an FDA advisory committee to reject Roche’s (OTCQX:RHHBY) lymphoma therapy Columvi, recently questioning the applicability of a trial that enrolled over 50% from regions outside Asia.

“We may speculate that the FDA is not going to be prepared to accept Summit's HARMONi data, which includes an even lower proportion of ex-U.S. patients, as "applicable,” the analyst wrote, referring to the study’s ex-Asia participation of 38%.  

“I am not sure today's readout is enough to secure approval in the U.S,” Ingham added.

data.title: SA analyst upgrades: ASML, UPST
data.description:
Here are the latest stock rating upgrades by Seeking Alpha analysts:

ASML (NASDAQ:ASML). Hold to buy by Khaveen Investments. "ASML is set for a strong rebound in 2025, with projected revenue growth of 20.3% after a contraction in 2024…With an attractive valuation after a 22% stock price decline, we upgrade ASML to a Strong Buy rating."

Upstart (NASDAQ:UPST). Hold to buy by Daniel Jones. "While risks remain, especially with economic uncertainty, Upstart's growth trajectory and improving profitability present a compelling long-term opportunity."

data.title: Rivian Automotive may raise $2B in the bond market to help fund its growth
Starz Entertainment adds 530K new subscribers
data.description:
• Starz Entertainment (NASDAQ:STRZ) added 530K new subscribers in its fiscal fourth quarter, contributing to $330.6M in revenue and $93.3M in operating income before depreciation and amortization (“OIBDA”).
  • Wall Street expected the company to report $326M in revenue.
  • For the fiscal year, total revenue reached $1.37B, which also exceeded estimates of $1.32B, with adjusted OIBDA of $201.5M, above the company’s expectations of $200M.
  • This is the company’s first quarterly results as a publicly traded company having split from Lionsgate (LION) on May 6. Beginning with the quarter ending June 30, Starz Entertainment (NASDAQ:STRZ) will begin reporting earnings per share.
  • In addition, the company has adopted a change to its fiscal year end to December 31 from March 31.
  • Shares rallied by as much as 24% on Friday to a post-IPO high of $20.50.

data.title: Shoe Carnival signals accelerated Shoe Station expansion to over 80% of fleet by March 2027 as rebanner strategy drives outsized growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Shoe Carnival, Inc. (SCVL) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Mark Worden opened the call stating that "the company's first quarter 2025 results were better than expected, with profits outperforming expectations by approximately 10%, our rebanner expansion plans delivering outstanding results and our debt free balance sheet is getting even stronger." Worden highlighted a "cautiously optimistic" stance for the back-to-school season, noting a compelling assortment and stable product costs.
  • Worden emphasized the company's singular focus to become "the nation's leading footwear retailer for families" and outlined Shoe Carnival's superior position due to lack of wholesale business and direct manufacturing exposure. He reaffirmed annual profit guidance.
  • Shoe Station achieved industry-leading growth, with sales up 4.9% for the quarter, while Shoe Carnival sales declined in line with industry trends. Worden stated, "Shoe Station is outpacing the industry and Shoe Carnival quarter-after-quarter for over two years now."
  • Worden announced an accelerated and ambitious expansion: "Shoe Station will represent over 80% of our store fleet from March 2027, up from our previous target of 51%. We're accelerating our investment to maximize this rollout before back to school 2026. By July 2026 at least 51% of our current store fleet will operate a Shoe Station."
  • CFO Patrick Edwards said, "I'm pleased to report that despite the challenging macroeconomic and retail environment, our first quarter profits outperformed market expectations by approximately 10%. While our profits are down compared to last year, this reflects our deliberate decision to invest in the rebanner initiative that Mark just outlined, a choice that is already showing promising returns through Shoe Station's exceptional performance and our continued balance sheet strength."

Outlook

  • Management reaffirmed annual fiscal 2025 guidance for net sales of $1.15 billion to $1.23 billion, GAAP EPS in a range of $1.60 to $2.10, gross profit margins of 35% to 36%, SG&A between $350 million and $360 million, and CapEx of $45 million to $60 million, with $30 million to $40 million for rebanners. For Q2, guidance is net sales of $310 million to $320 million and EPS of $0.55 to $0.65.
  • Edwards noted, "We expect a moderating trend in our sales declines in the back half of the year. This moderating decline results from our rebanner strategy and continued success in event period shopping."

Financial Results

  • First quarter net income was $9.3 million or $0.34 per diluted share, compared to $17.3 million or $0.63 per diluted share in Q1 2024, which Edwards attributed to planned investments in the rebanner strategy and broader industry headwinds.
  • Net sales for Q1 2025 were $277.7 million, down 7.5% from $300.4 million last year. Comparable store sales declined 8.1%.
  • Shoe Station sales increased 4.9% and were comp positive. Rogan's achieved net sales above $19 million. Shoe Carnival sales declined 10%.
  • Gross profit margin was 34.5%, down 110 basis points from Q1 2024, mainly due to deleverage from lower sales and rebanner costs, partially offset by a 50 basis point increase in merchandise margins.
  • The company ended the quarter with $93 million in cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities, up $23.5 million from last year, and maintained a debt-free position.

Q&A

  • Sam Poser, Williams Trading, asked about the drivers of Shoe Station's outperformance and competitive positioning. Mark Worden explained, "For Shoe Station, we see a lot of white space nationally where the competitive set is not completely fulfilling what that higher end customer wants."
  • Poser followed up on private label and brand mix, to which Worden replied, "We are a house of brands. Shoe Station is a house of the best brands...private label will be a small place to fill in, maybe a price point, maybe a promotional point or something unique, but we will have the best brands at high AURs and robust margins."
  • Mitchel Kummetz, Seaport Research Partners, focused on rebanner strategy payback and guidance. CFO Edwards confirmed cost acceleration for rebanners, stating, "We do anticipate that there will be more cost beyond the $22 million to $27 million that we've disclosed."
  • Kummetz also probed on tariff impacts; Worden responded, "To date, we've had nothing that surprised us. That could change tomorrow. This is very fluid. But as I said here today Mitch, we've seen no cost or price change that materially impact how we think about the shape of the year."
  • Jim Chartier, Monness, Crespi, Hardt & Co, asked about sales trends by month. Worden responded that "February was tough...March, April combined was okay...Carnival did poorly across the months. Station was exciting. Rogan's delivered just as we thought."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was generally neutral with focused inquiries on the sustainability of rebanner outperformance, the impact of tariffs, and clarity on private label strategy.
  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone, particularly regarding the rebanner initiative and Shoe Station's growth, with Worden frequently using phrases like "cautiously optimistic" and expressing strong conviction in the store transformation plan.
  • Compared to the prior quarter, management's confidence has increased, particularly in accelerating the Shoe Station rollout, while analysts' tone remains consistent, seeking clarity on risk factors and execution.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for FY25 is reaffirmed, with the upper end unchanged from last quarter. The new, more aggressive goal is for Shoe Station to reach over 80% of the fleet by March 2027, up from the previously announced 51%.
  • The pace of rebanner conversions has accelerated, with all 75 planned for FY25 now scheduled to be completed this year.
  • Analysts' focus continues to be on sustainability of outperformance, payback of investments, and tariff impacts. The management tone shifted from cautious optimism to a more assertive and ambitious stance.
  • Key metrics such as net sales, gross margin, and net income are lower year-over-year, reflecting planned investments and ongoing industry headwinds.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited continued industry-wide pressures on lower income consumers and soft consumer confidence as ongoing challenges.
  • Worden acknowledged, "We have not yet experienced nor do we have visibility to any massive product cost or price increases outside of ranges considered in our guidance. This could evolve, but that is the situation now."
  • The company maintains deliberately higher inventory as a hedge against supply chain uncertainties, planning to adjust as risk subsides.
  • Tariff and vendor pricing volatility remain fluid risks, with management ready to respond if conditions change.

Final Takeaway

Shoe Carnival’s Q1 2025 call underscores the company’s accelerated investment in transforming its store base, with plans for Shoe Station to represent over 80% of the fleet by March 2027. The rebanner strategy is already delivering outsized sales and margin improvements, supporting management’s reaffirmed guidance despite industry challenges and planned near-term profit dilution. Strong cash reserves, a debt-free balance sheet, and compelling performance at Shoe Station provide the foundation for continued expansion and potential market share gains, while management remains alert to evolving risks around consumer sentiment and supply chain costs.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Wall Street falls at the end of a massive May on U.S.-China trade drama
data.description:
Wall Street on Friday was on track to post a strong monthly advance, though sentiment was subdued on the final day of May after U.S. President Donald Trump said China had "totally violated" a trade truce. 

An in-line reading of a key inflation gauge and an improvement in consumer sentiment were not enough to lift the mood.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last -0.7% in afternoon trade, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -1.2%, and the Dow (DJI) seesawed, last -0.4%. On a monthly basis, the S&P was up nearly 6%. 

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was down 1 basis point at 4.41%, and the 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 3 basis points at 3.92%.

The U.S. and China at the start of the month entered into a surprise trade agreement, under which they agreed to decrease tariff rates against each other for 90 days. But Trump on Friday said that China had violated that agreement. 

The President's accusation comes a day after legal back-and-forth over his "Liberation Day" tariffs. The U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit on Thursday issued an administrative stay blocking a ruling from a lower court on Wednesday that struck down the reciprocal tariffs imposed in April.  

“We expect the markets to remain headline-driven, especially headlines related to tariffs, of which there are many. As we start to see more clarity on trade, we expect more traditional drivers of markets, like earnings and fundamentals, to lead the way and drive sentiment and the trajectory of stocks,” said Clark Bellin, president and CIO at Bellwether Wealth.

Turning to the U.S. economic calendar, the Fed's preferred inflation gauge rose 0.1% in April, as expected.

“Friday's PCE shows that inflation is continuing to soften, which is encouraging news, especially as the economy prepares for higher prices due to tariffs. It will take time for tariffs to work their way through the economy, so we are particularly paying close attention to the inflation readings in 2-3 months from now to better understand how tariffs are affecting consumer prices,” Bellin said.

“Even with a softer PCE reading, it's still above the Fed's 2% target, and we expect the Fed to remain on hold when it comes to interest rates for at least the next six months,” he added.

Also, the U.S. trade deficit narrowed in April. Additionally, Chicago PMI data slid further into contraction territory in May.

data.title: Bitcoin on course to see weekly fall as tariff talks dominate headlines again
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is set to fall 2.9% on a weekly basis but stayed firm above the $100K mark, having traded above it throughout the week.

The cryptocurrency had hit an all-time high last week and had hovered around that level on Tuesday.

The world’s largest digital currency, however, saw a dip as President Donald Trump’s tariffs once again took center stage.

“Traders are optimistic. On one hand, this past weekend's market dip showed us how quickly cryptocurrencies could fall from Trump's sudden trade threats. On the other hand, the speedy extension of tariff deadlines just this morning reinforces the widely held belief that the worst of the trade war is over,” said Jeff Mei, COO at BTSE.

That being said, given the unpredictability of these trade talks, traders are still closely observing as they are gradually accumulating positions, Mei added.

On Wednesday, a federal trade court has struck down the "reciprocal" tariffs that Trump imposed on what he called "Liberation Day," putting an immediate stop to the collection of a sweeping set of levies that the president imposed.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) on that day fell as low as $106.8K.

The tariff driven volatility continued, as the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit on Thursday issued an administrative stay blocking a ruling from a lower court on Wednesday that struck down "reciprocal" tariffs Trump imposed in April.

“Bitcoin has proven itself as a store of value, diversification tool, and monetary hedge, especially in today's macro environment,” Seeking Alpha analyst James Foord said.

With U.S. debt at unsustainable levels, monetary inflation is likely, making Bitcoin an essential hedge against fiscal irresponsibility. I remain highly bullish and advise investors to accumulate Bitcoin as a long-term hold for protection and growth, Foord added.

Despite the weekly fall, few other analysts also remained bullish on the cryptocurrency’s prospects.

“We remain optimistic for the long-term prospects of the markets as institutions continue to integrate further into the crypto industry, and the entry of institutional players also signifies broader adoption of RWA,” Kay Lu, CEO, HashKey Eco Labs said.

More Notable NewsThe iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF (IBIT), managed by BlackRock, has emerged as a powerhouse in the ETF world, drawing massive investor attention as Bitcoin (BTC-USD) hovers near record highs.

DeFi Technologies (DEFT) on Thursday announced its strategic collaboration with Misyon Bank and its crypto trading subsidiary Misyon Kripto to prepare for introducing a series of innovative digital investment products to Turkish investors.

Trading and investing platform eToro (ETOR) added 12 more crypto assets to its U.S. platform to the three it already had — Bitcoin (BTC-USD), Bitcoin cash (BCH-USD), and Ether (ETH-USD).

GameStop Corp. (GME) said it had purchased 4,710 Bitcoin (BTC-USD). The approximate market value of the company's Bitcoin position is $513.0 million.

Block (XYZ) will launch Bitcoin (BTC-USD) payments on Square, its payment system for merchants, making bitcoin more accessible and usable.

U.S. President Donald Trump's company behind the Truth Social app—Trump Media & Technology Group (DJT)—is reportedly planning to raise up to $3 billion to invest in cryptocurrencies.

Crypto Moves

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) was down 2.1% to $104.3K on Friday as at 2:11pm ET. Ether (ETH-USD) fell 4.2% to $2.6K. Dogecoin (DOGE-USD) was 10.5% lower.
